import type { ReactNode } from 'react';

export type FileType =
  | 'folder'
  | 'document'
  | 'code'
  | 'image'
  | 'pdf'
  | 'archive'
  | 'spreadsheet'
  | 'presentation';

export interface FileEntry {
  id: string;
  name: string;
  path: string;
  fileType: FileType;
  size: number;
  modifiedAt: Date;
}

function FileCodeIcon() {
  return (
    <svg width="20" height="20" viewBox="0 0 20 20" fill="none">
      <rect x="3" y="2" width="14" height="16" rx="2" fill="#5856D6" />
      <polyline
        points="7,8 5,10 7,12"
        stroke="#FFFFFF"
        strokeWidth="1.3"
        strokeLinecap="round"
        strokeLinejoin="round"
        fill="none"
      />
      <polyline
        points="13,8 15,10 13,12"
        stroke="#FFFFFF"
        strokeWidth="1.3"
        strokeLinecap="round"
        strokeLinejoin="round"
        fill="none"
      />
      <line
        x1="11"
        y1="7"
        x2="9"
        y2="13"
        stroke="#FFFFFF"
        strokeWidth="1.3"
        strokeLinecap="round"
      />
    </svg>
  );
}

function FileImageIcon() {
  return (
    <svg width="20" height="20" viewBox="0 0 20 20" fill="none">
      <rect x="3" y="2" width="14" height="16" rx="2" fill="#30D158" />
      <circle cx="8" cy="8" r="1.5" fill="#FFFFFF" />
      <path
        d="M3 14l4-4 2.5 2.5L13 9l4 5v1a2 2 0 01-2 2H5a2 2 0 01-2-2v-1z"
        fill="rgba(255,255,255,0.4)"
      />
    </svg>
  );
}

function FilePdfIcon() {
  return (
    <svg width="20" height="20" viewBox="0 0 20 20" fill="none">
      <rect x="3" y="2" width="14" height="16" rx="2" fill="#FF3B30" />
      <text
        x="10"
        y="12.5"
        textAnchor="middle"
        fill="#FFFFFF"
        fontSize="6"
        fontWeight="700"
        fontFamily="Inter, system-ui, sans-serif"
      >
        PDF
      </text>
    </svg>
  );
}

function FileArchiveIcon() {
  return (
    <svg width="20" height="20" viewBox="0 0 20 20" fill="none">
      <rect x="3" y="2" width="14" height="16" rx="2" fill="#8E8E93" />
      <rect
        x="9"
        y="4"
        width="2"
        height="2"
        rx="0.5"
        fill="rgba(255,255,255,0.6)"
      />
      <rect
        x="9"
        y="7"
        width="2"
        height="2"
        rx="0.5"
        fill="rgba(255,255,255,0.6)"
      />
      <rect
        x="9"
        y="10"
        width="2"
        height="2"
        rx="0.5"
        fill="rgba(255,255,255,0.6)"
      />
      <rect
        x="8"
        y="13"
        width="4"
        height="3"
        rx="1"
        stroke="#FFFFFF"
        strokeWidth="1"
        fill="none"
      />
    </svg>
  );
}

function FileSpreadsheetIcon() {
  return (
    <svg width="20" height="20" viewBox="0 0 20 20" fill="none">
      <rect x="3" y="2" width="14" height="16" rx="2" fill="#34C759" />
      <line
        x1="3"
        y1="7"
        x2="17"
        y2="7"
        stroke="rgba(255,255,255,0.4)"
        strokeWidth="1"
      />
      <line
        x1="3"
        y1="11"
        x2="17"
        y2="11"
        stroke="rgba(255,255,255,0.4)"
        strokeWidth="1"
      />
      <line
        x1="3"
        y1="15"
        x2="17"
        y2="15"
        stroke="rgba(255,255,255,0.4)"
        strokeWidth="1"
      />
      <line
        x1="8"
        y1="2"
        x2="8"
        y2="18"
        stroke="rgba(255,255,255,0.4)"
        strokeWidth="1"
      />
    </svg>
  );
}

function FilePresentationIcon() {
  return (
    <svg width="20" height="20" viewBox="0 0 20 20" fill="none">
      <rect x="3" y="2" width="14" height="16" rx="2" fill="#FF9500" />
      <rect
        x="5.5"
        y="5"
        width="9"
        height="6"
        rx="1"
        stroke="#FFFFFF"
        strokeWidth="1.2"
        fill="none"
      />
      <line
        x1="10"
        y1="11"
        x2="10"
        y2="14"
        stroke="#FFFFFF"
        strokeWidth="1.2"
        strokeLinecap="round"
      />
      <line
        x1="7.5"
        y1="14"
        x2="12.5"
        y2="14"
        stroke="#FFFFFF"
        strokeWidth="1.2"
        strokeLinecap="round"
      />
    </svg>
  );
}

function FileFolderIcon() {
  return (
    <svg width="20" height="20" viewBox="0 0 20 20" fill="none">
      <path
        d="M2 6a2 2 0 012-2h4l2 2h6a2 2 0 012 2v7a2 2 0 01-2 2H4a2 2 0 01-2-2V6z"
        fill="#339AF0"
      />
      <path
        d="M2 8a0 0 0 000 0h16v7a2 2 0 01-2 2H4a2 2 0 01-2-2V8z"
        fill="#1C7ED6"
      />
    </svg>
  );
}

function FileDocumentIcon() {
  return (
    <svg width="20" height="20" viewBox="0 0 20 20" fill="none">
      <rect x="3" y="2" width="14" height="16" rx="2" fill="#007AFF" />
      <line
        x1="6"
        y1="7"
        x2="14"
        y2="7"
        stroke="#FFFFFF"
        strokeWidth="1.2"
        strokeLinecap="round"
      />
      <line
        x1="6"
        y1="10"
        x2="14"
        y2="10"
        stroke="#FFFFFF"
        strokeWidth="1.2"
        strokeLinecap="round"
      />
      <line
        x1="6"
        y1="13"
        x2="11"
        y2="13"
        stroke="#FFFFFF"
        strokeWidth="1.2"
        strokeLinecap="round"
      />
    </svg>
  );
}

export function getFileIcon(fileType: FileType): ReactNode {
  switch (fileType) {
    case 'folder':
      return <FileFolderIcon />;
    case 'code':
      return <FileCodeIcon />;
    case 'image':
      return <FileImageIcon />;
    case 'pdf':
      return <FilePdfIcon />;
    case 'archive':
      return <FileArchiveIcon />;
    case 'spreadsheet':
      return <FileSpreadsheetIcon />;
    case 'presentation':
      return <FilePresentationIcon />;
    case 'document':
      return <FileDocumentIcon />;
  }
}

export function FileSearchCommandIcon() {
  return (
    <svg width="20" height="20" viewBox="0 0 20 20" fill="none">
      <defs>
        <linearGradient id="filesearch-bg" x1="0" y1="0" x2="0" y2="1">
          <stop offset="0%" stopColor="#5AC8FA" />
          <stop offset="100%" stopColor="#007AFF" />
        </linearGradient>
      </defs>
      <rect
        x="1"
        y="1"
        width="18"
        height="18"
        rx="4"
        fill="url(#filesearch-bg)"
      />
      <path
        d="M6 4.5h3.5L12 7v6.5a1 1 0 01-1 1H6a1 1 0 01-1-1V5.5a1 1 0 011-1z"
        stroke="#FFFFFF"
        strokeWidth="1.2"
        strokeLinecap="round"
        strokeLinejoin="round"
        fill="none"
      />
      <path
        d="M9.5 4.5V7H12"
        stroke="#FFFFFF"
        strokeWidth="1.2"
        strokeLinecap="round"
        strokeLinejoin="round"
        fill="none"
      />
      <circle
        cx="12.5"
        cy="12.5"
        r="3"
        stroke="#FFFFFF"
        strokeWidth="1.2"
        fill="none"
      />
      <line
        x1="14.6"
        y1="14.6"
        x2="16"
        y2="16"
        stroke="#FFFFFF"
        strokeWidth="1.2"
        strokeLinecap="round"
      />
    </svg>
  );
}

- [IN_PROGRESS] [P1] TAURI006 - Add native file system access. Use @tauri-apps/api/fs
  and @tauri-apps/plugin-fs for real file operations. Replace mock file
  search with real directory scanning. Create Rust command for fast
  recursive file search (ignore node_modules, .git, hidden dirs by default).
  Index common directories (~/, ~/Documents, ~/Downloads, ~/Desktop).
  Return file metadata (size, modified date, type). Wire into FileSearchView
  to replace mock data. Add "Open" action that uses @tauri-apps/api/shell
  to open files with default app.
  Sub-tasks:
  - [ ] TAURI006a - Rust-side: Add walkdir + tauri-plugin-shell dependencies.
    Create search_files Tauri command with recursive dir traversal, .git/
    node_modules/hidden exclusion, extension-based file type detection, file
    metadata (name, path, size, modified date, file type). Add open_file and
    reveal_in_finder Tauri commands. Files: Cargo.toml, lib.rs.
  - [ ] TAURI006b - Update capabilities/default.json with shell:allow-open
    permission. Install @tauri-apps/plugin-shell JS package via bun.
  - [ ] TAURI006c - Extend PlatformAPI: add PlatformFiles interface (searchFiles,
    openFile, revealInFinder, moveToTrash) to types.ts. Implement in tauri.ts
    (invoke Tauri commands) and web.ts (mock/noop fallbacks). Export from index.ts.
  - [ ] TAURI006d - Refactor FileSearchView to use PlatformAPI for real file search
    in Tauri mode, mock data in web mode. Wire open/reveal/trash actions to real
    platform operations. Add loading state during search.

     border-radius ($radius-sm: 4px, $radius-md: 8px, $radius-lg: 12px,
     $radius-xl: 16px),

- [x] UXDS001 - The design is very different from Raycast, colors,

  actions/index.ts. All 6 views (App.tsx, ClipboardHistoryView,
  EmojiPickerView, SnippetManagerView, FileSearchView, ColorPickerView)
  refactored: dropdown copy actions use createCopyAction, keyboard shortcut
  handlers use performCopy, all imports via barrel export. SettingsView
  updated to import CopyHUDIcon from barrel. Zero direct navigator.clipboard
  calls outside clipboard.ts. All 93 tests pass, bun run check passes.
  Sub-tasks:
  - [x] COMP016a - Create src/utils/clipboard.ts with copyToClipboard(text)
    utility and src/components/action_panel/actions/copy_to_clipboard.tsx with
    createCopyAction factory function and shared CopyHUDIcon.
  - [x] COMP016b - Replace ad-hoc clipboard/copy handlers in all views
    (EmojiPicker, ClipboardHistory, SnippetManager, FileSearch, ColorPicker,
    App.tsx) with createCopyAction. Remove duplicated HUD icons.

- [x] [P1] COMP017 - Build Action.OpenInBrowser declarative action. Opens
  URL in default browser. Accepts: url, title (default "Open in Browser"),
  shortcut. In web: window.open(url). In Tauri: @tauri-apps/api/shell open.
  Shows HUD "Opened in Browser". Create open_in_browser.tsx in actions/.
  Done: Created src/utils/open_url.ts with openUrl(url) async utility —
  validates URL scheme (http/https only, blocks javascript:/data: URIs),
  uses window.open for web (future Tauri plugin integration point). Created
  src/components/action_panel/actions/open_in_browser.tsx with
  OpenInBrowserHUDIcon (external link SVG), performOpen() for imperative calls
  (keyboard shortcuts), and createOpenInBrowserAction() factory for declarative
  dropdown action definitions. Extracted shared ShowHUD type to
  actions/types.ts (used by both copy_to_clipboard and open_in_browser,
  eliminates duplication). Barrel export in actions/index.ts. ClipboardHistoryView
  updated: "Open in Browser" action shown conditionally for link-type entries,
  ⌘O keyboard shortcut via useKeyboardShortcut. FileSearchView updated:
  replaced local OpenHUDIcon with shared OpenInBrowserHUDIcon. All 93 tests
  pass, bun run check passes.

- [x] [P1] COMP018 - Build List.Item.Detail inline detail panel. When a
  ListItem has a detail prop, the list splits into list (left, ~40%) and
  detail panel (right, ~60%) showing the item's detail content. Reuses
  Detail component. Toggle detail panel visibility with an action or
  automatic on selection. Animate panel slide-in. Update List component
  to accept showDetail prop. Create list_item_detail.scss for split layout.
  Done: Added `detail` prop (ReactNode) to List component. When `detail` is
  provided, List renders a `.list-detail-split` flex container with list at
  $list-detail-ratio (40%) and detail panel at flex:1. Detail panel derived
  internally from `detail != null` (no separate `showDetail` boolean to
  prevent invalid state). `.list-detail-panel` has display:flex/flex-direction:
  column for proper flex chain with Detail component. Slide-in animation
  (translateX 20px + opacity) with prefers-reduced-motion: reduce fallback.
  ARIA: role="complementary" aria-label="Item detail" on panel wrapper.
  Refactored App.tsx root view to use List's `detail` prop instead of manual
  split layout at command-palette__body level. Removed obsolete
  `.command-palette__body--has-detail` modifier from command_palette.scss and
  detail.scss. $list-detail-ratio token added to _tokens.scss. All 93 tests
  pass, bun run check passes.

- [ ] [P2] COMP019 - Build Action.PickDate component. Date picker that
  appears inline in action panel or as a pushed form field. Supports
  date-only and date+time modes. Min/max date constraints. Calendar grid
  UI with month/year navigation. Selected date highlighted with accent.
  Keyboard navigation: arrows for days, PageUp/Down for months. Create
  src/components/date_picker/ with date_picker.tsx and date_picker.scss.

- [ ] [P2] COMP020 - Add List pagination support (infinite scroll). Add
  onLoadMore callback and hasMore boolean props to List. When user scrolls
  near bottom (IntersectionObserver on sentinel element), trigger
  onLoadMore. Show loading skeleton items (3 placeholder rows with pulse
  animation) while loading. Update List component. Demo: Clipboard History
  loads 20 items at a time.

- [ ] [P2] COMP021 - Build proper markdown renderer for Detail component.
  Replace current simple text rendering with proper CommonMark markdown
  using existing marked + dompurify dependencies (already in package.json).
  Support: headings, bold, italic, code (inline + blocks with syntax
  highlighting via CSS classes), links, lists (ordered + unordered),
  blockquotes, tables, horizontal rules, images (as placeholders in web).
  Create src/utils/markdown.ts as a thin wrapper around marked+dompurify.
  Add markdown-specific SCSS styles in detail.scss. Update Detail component
  to render parsed markdown.

- [ ] [P2] COMP022 - Build Form.LinkAccessory component. Small accessory
  displayed in the right side of the SearchBar/navigation bar when a Form
  is active. Shows a link with icon + label. Clickable, opens URL or
  triggers action. Create src/components/form/form_link_accessory.tsx.
  Update SearchBar to accept optional rightAccessory prop. Demo: "View Docs"
  link in Create Snippet form.

- [ ] [P3] COMP023 - Build color swatch accessory for ListItem. When a
  ListItem accessory has a color hex value, render a small color circle
  (12x12px, rounded) next to the text. Used in clipboard history for copied
  colors and in color picker. Update ListItem rendering to detect color
  accessory type and render swatch. Add color?: string field to
  ListItemAccessoryData.

- [ ] [P3] COMP024 - Build empty search fallback actions. When root search
  has no matching results, show fallback items: "Search Google for '{query}'",
  "Search DuckDuckGo for '{query}'", "Search Wikipedia for '{query}'". Each
  item has appropriate search engine icon. Enter opens search URL in browser.
  Configurable fallback list in settings. Create src/data/fallback_data.ts.
  Update App.tsx to show fallbacks when filteredSections is empty.

=== VISUAL POLISH ===

- [ ] [P2] VIS001 - Add virtual scrolling for large lists. Current List
  renders all items in DOM which becomes slow with 100+ items. Implement
  virtual scroll: only render visible items + buffer (5 items above/below
  viewport). Calculate item positions from fixed height ($list-item-height).
  Maintain scroll position on item count changes. Keep keyboard navigation
  working (scrollIntoView on active item). Create src/hooks/use_virtual_
  scroll.ts. Apply to List component. Test with 1000+ items in Clipboard
  History.

- [ ] [P2] VIS002 - Add custom window title bar. Since Tauri uses frameless
  window, build a custom title bar. macOS style: traffic light buttons
  (close/minimize/maximize) on left side, centered title text. Make it
  draggable (data-tauri-drag-region). In web mode, hide title bar (browser
  has its own). Create src/components/title_bar/ with title_bar.tsx and
  title_bar.scss. Position above CommandPalette. 28px height matching macOS.

- [ ] [P2] VIS003 - Add smooth scroll momentum and snap-to-item. Lists
  should have native-feeling scroll momentum (CSS scroll-behavior: smooth).
  When scroll stops, snap to nearest item boundary (CSS scroll-snap-type:
  y proximity on list container, scroll-snap-align: start on items). Ensure
  keyboard navigation scrollIntoView uses smooth behavior. Test on macOS
  trackpad and mouse wheel.

- [ ] [P2] VIS004 - Add focus ring animation matching Raycast. When
  navigating list items with keyboard, show a subtle animated focus ring
  that transitions between items (not just instant highlight switch).
  Use CSS transition on a pseudo-element that moves between item positions.
  The ring should have a very subtle glow (box-shadow with accent color at
  10% opacity). 80ms transition duration. Only show on keyboard navigation,
  not mouse hover.

- [ ] [P3] VIS005 - Add window open/close animations. When CommandPalette
  appears: macOS-style spring animation (scale from 0.95 to 1.0, opacity
  0 to 1, with slight overshoot). When closing: scale to 0.97, opacity to 0,
  faster (150ms). Use CSS @keyframes with cubic-bezier easing that mimics
  spring physics. In Tauri mode, coordinate with window show/hide timing.

- [ ] [P3] VIS006 - Add Grid view hover preview tooltip. When hovering a
  GridItem for 800ms, show an enlarged preview tooltip above the item
  (larger icon/image, full title, metadata). Positioned above the item,
  centered. Fade in/out. Dismiss on mouse leave. Create
  src/components/grid/grid_preview.tsx and grid_preview.scss.

- [ ] [P3] VIS007 - Add subtle parallax effect to command palette background.
  Very subtle background gradient shift based on mouse position (±2px max
  movement). Creates depth illusion matching Raycast's glass effect. CSS
  custom properties updated via mousemove listener (throttled to rAF). Only
  affects background layer, not content. Disable when prefers-reduced-motion.

=== SEARCH & INTERACTION ===

- [x] [P1] SRCH001 - Implement fuzzy search matching. Replace current
  substring search with fuzzy matching (Fuse.js-style scoring). Match
  characters don't need to be contiguous: "clhist" matches "Clipboard
  History". Score based on: consecutive matches (bonus), match at word
  boundary (bonus), match position (earlier = better), gap penalty. Highlight
  matched characters in results (bold or underline). Create
  src/utils/fuzzy_search.ts with score() and match() functions. Apply to
  root search and all view search bars.
  Done: Recursive-descent fuzzy scorer with greedy fallback when recursion
  cap (10k calls) is reached. Scoring: consecutive match (+8), word boundary
  (+10), first char (+12), case match (+1), position bonus (1-idx/len)*2,
  exact match (+50), gap penalties (-3 start, -1 per). Word boundary detection
  handles separators, camelCase, and digit-letter transitions. HighlightedText
  component accepts optional pre-computed indices to avoid double scoring.
  Uses <span> (not <mark>) for accessibility. SCSS uses $font-weight-semibold
  token. command_registry.ts searchCommands() scores and sorts by relevance.
  All 4 view search bars (Clipboard, Snippets, Emoji, Files) use fuzzyMatch
  for filtering and pass query to ListItem for highlighting. App.tsx root
  search passes query to ListItem. All 93 tests pass, bun run check passes.
  Sub-tasks:
  - [x] SRCH001a - Create src/utils/fuzzy_search.ts with fuzzyScore() and
    fuzzyMatch() functions. Score: consecutive match bonus, word boundary bonus,
    early position bonus, gap penalty. Return matched indices for highlighting.
  - [x] SRCH001b - Create HighlightedText component (src/components/highlighted_text/)
    that renders text with matched characters highlighted (bold). Takes text + query.
  - [x] SRCH001c - Update command_registry.ts searchCommands() to use fuzzy search
    with scoring and result sorting by score.
  - [x] SRCH001d - Update ListItem to accept optional query prop, use HighlightedText
    for title and subtitle rendering. Update App.tsx to pass query to ListItem.
  - [x] SRCH001e - Update all view search bars (ClipboardHistoryView,
    SnippetManagerView, EmojiPickerView, FileSearchView) to use fuzzy search
    for filtering and pass query for highlighting.

- [x] [P1] SRCH002 - Add recent commands tracking. Track last 10 opened
  commands with timestamps. Show "Recent Commands" section in root search
  when query is empty (above other sections). Persist via localStorage/
  SQLite. When user opens a command (drill-in), add to recents. Deduplicate.
  Cap at 10. Create src/hooks/use_recent_commands.ts. Wire into App.tsx
  root search sections.
  Done: Created useRecentCommands hook (src/hooks/use_recent_commands.ts)
  following useFavorites pattern. localStorage persistence via storage.ts
  with "recent-commands" key. Max 10 items, most-recent-first ordering,
  deduplication on add. App.tsx integration: addRecent called only for
  commands with components (not detail-only or fallback items). Tray
  navigation also tracks recents. "Recent Commands" section shown in root
  search when query is empty, below Favorites, above registry sections.
  Items in Favorites excluded from Recent section. allFlatItems built from
  unfiltered registrySections to prevent filter-dependent disappearance.
  clearRecent exported for future UI integration. All 93 tests pass, bun
  run check passes.

- [ ] [P2] SRCH003 - Add search bar placeholder cycling. When search bar
  is empty, cycle through helpful placeholder texts every 4 seconds:
  "Type a command...", "Search applications...", "Calculate something...",
  "Find files...". Smooth fade transition between placeholders. Stop cycling
  when user starts typing. Resume on clear. Use CSS animation for opacity
  transitions. Update SearchBar component.

- [ ] [P2] SRCH004 - Add ⌘L to copy current item URL/value. Universal
  shortcut that copies the most relevant value of the selected item (URL
  for links, path for files, content for snippets, hex for colors). Show
  HUD with what was copied. Register via useKeyboardShortcut. Works across
  all views.

- [ ] [P2] SRCH005 - Add search debouncing and loading state. Debounce
  search input by 150ms to avoid excessive re-renders during fast typing.
  Show loading bar during debounce period. Create src/hooks/use_debounce.ts
  with generic useDebounce(value, delay) hook. Apply to all search bars.
  Cancel pending debounce on unmount.

- [ ] [P3] SRCH006 - Add Tab completion in search. When Tab is pressed in
  search and there's a single matching result, auto-complete the search
  query to match that result's title. If multiple matches share a common
  prefix, complete to that prefix. Visual: briefly highlight the completed
  portion. Works in root search only (not within views). Prevent Tab from
  focusing other elements during search.

=== INFRASTRUCTURE ===

- [x] [P1] INFRA004 - Build extension architecture. Formalize each screen
  as an "extension" with a manifest. Create ExtensionManifest type in
  src/registry/types.ts: id, name, icon, description, author, commands[].
  Each command has: name, title, subtitle, keywords, mode (view/no-view/
  menu-bar), component. Refactor existing registry to use extension manifests.
  Create src/extensions/ directory with one folder per extension (clipboard/,
  snippets/, emoji/, etc). Each extension exports its manifest. Auto-discover
  extensions via barrel import.
  Done: Created ExtensionManifest (id, name, icon, description, author,
  commands[]) and ExtensionCommand (name, title, subtitle, icon, keywords,
  aliases, mode, component, fullView, section, accessories, detail) types in
  types.ts. CommandMode union type: 'view' | 'no-view' | 'menu-bar'. Added
  extensionId to CommandRegistration for traceability. registerExtension() in
  command_registry.ts maps manifest commands to registerCommand calls (cmd.name
  as ID for backward compatibility). Dev-mode duplicate ID warning via
  import.meta.env.DEV. Created 12 extension manifests in src/extensions/:
  clipboard, snippets, file_search, quicklinks, emoji, window_management,
  color_picker, settings, calculator, dictionary, system_monitor, applications
  (5 app commands). Barrel import in extensions/index.ts auto-registers all
  extensions. Section order preserved (Suggestions→Commands→Applications).
  Deleted old registry/commands.ts. Registry index.ts imports from extensions
  instead. All 16 command IDs preserved exactly. bun run check passes.
  Sub-tasks:
  - [x] INFRA004a - Create ExtensionManifest and ExtensionCommand types in
    types.ts. Add registerExtension() to command_registry.ts. Export from index.ts.
  - [x] INFRA004b - Create src/extensions/ directory with manifest files for
    each built-in extension: clipboard, snippets, file-search, quicklinks,
    emoji, window-management, color-picker, settings, and applications bundle.
  - [x] INFRA004c - Replace commands.ts with barrel import from extensions/,
    create extensions/index.ts for auto-discovery. Clean up dead code.

- [x] [P1] INFRA005 - Create Tauri IPC bridge layer. Abstract all
  platform-specific operations behind a unified interface. Create
  src/platform/index.ts with PlatformAPI interface: clipboard (read/write/
  monitor), files (search/open/trash), apps (list/launch), shell (execute),
  window (show/hide/position), notifications (send). Two implementations:
  src/platform/web.ts (browser fallbacks using navigator, localStorage, etc)
  and src/platform/tauri.ts (real native via @tauri-apps/api). Auto-detect
  environment and export correct implementation. All views import from
  platform/index.ts only.
  Done: PlatformAPI interface with PlatformClipboard, PlatformShell, and
  PlatformWindow interfaces in src/platform/types.ts. Web implementation
  (src/platform/web.ts) uses navigator.clipboard, window.open, anchor clicks.
  Tauri implementation (src/platform/tauri.ts) uses dynamic imports for
  @tauri-apps/api/window, @tauri-apps/api/core, @tauri-apps/api/event.
  Auto-detection via __TAURI_INTERNALS__ check. Race-safe initPlatform() with
  cached promise. getPlatform() synchronous accessor (throws if Tauri not
  initialized). main.tsx awaits initPlatform() before createRoot. clipboard.ts,
  open_url.ts, use_window.ts refactored to delegate to getPlatform(). Shell
  methods are async (Promise<void>) for future native Tauri shell integration.
  Unsubscribe type for cleanup callbacks. isTauri exported from platform/index.ts
  and re-exported from use_window.ts for backward compatibility. Tauri clipboard/
  shell use web APIs as fallback (proper native implementations deferred to
  TAURI005-007). All 93 tests pass, bun run check passes.
  Sub-tasks:
  - [x] INFRA005a - Create platform API types and implementations
    (src/platform/types.ts, src/platform/web.ts, src/platform/tauri.ts,
    src/platform/index.ts). PlatformAPI covers: clipboard (writeText/readText),
    shell (openUrl/openDeepLink as async), window (hide/setPositionPref/
    onFocusChanged/onTrayNavigate/syncStoredPositionToBackend).
  - [x] INFRA005b - Refactor existing utils to use platform API: clipboard.ts,
    open_url.ts, use_window.ts, main.tsx. Consumers unchanged.

- [ ] [P2] INFRA006 - Add error boundary components. Create
  src/components/error_boundary/ with error_boundary.tsx (class component
  wrapping React.ErrorBoundary). Show friendly error UI: icon, "Something
  went wrong" title, error message (collapsed by default, expandable),
  "Try Again" button that resets state. Style matching Raycast error states.
  Wrap each pushed view in error boundary. Log errors to console with
  component stack. Create error_boundary.scss.

- [ ] [P2] INFRA007 - Add state management with @belt/tools state-machine.
Replace prop-drilling and scattered useState across App - belt/tools is an
internal package public available in npm. See docs and examples at /Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/belt/packages/tools

- [ ] [P2] INFRA008 - Add proper build configuration for production. Vite
  production build: minification (terser), tree-shaking, chunk splitting
  (vendor chunk for React, component chunks per route/view). Configure
  asset hashing for cache busting. Add bundle size analysis (rollup-plugin-
  visualizer). Set up path aliases (@/ for src/). Add environment variable
  support (.env, .env.production). Target: initial bundle < 200KB gzipped.
  Update vite.config.ts.

- [ ] [P1] INFRA009 - Add CI/CD pipeline. Create .github/workflows/ci.yml:
  lint (bun run check), test (bun run test), build (bun run build), optional
  Playwright visual tests. Run on push to main and PRs. Cache bun
  dependencies. Fail on lint errors or test failures. Add status badges
  to README. For Tauri: add tauri-build job that creates platform-specific
  binaries (macOS .dmg, Windows .msi, Linux .AppImage).

- [ ] [P3] INFRA010 - Add telemetry/analytics abstraction. Create
  src/utils/analytics.ts with track(event, properties?) function. Events:
  command_opened, search_query, action_triggered, extension_enabled/disabled,
  theme_changed. In web demo: log to console. In production: send to
  analytics endpoint. Respect user opt-out preference in settings. Add
  "Share Usage Data" toggle in Settings > Advanced. No PII in events.

- [ ] [P3] INFRA011 - Add i18n internationalization support. Create
  src/i18n/ with translation files (en.json, pt.json, es.json, ja.json).
  Translation keys for all UI strings (button labels, section titles,
  placeholder texts, error messages). Create useTranslation hook that
  returns t(key) function. Language selector in Settings > General. Default
  to system locale. Persist preference. Start with English strings
  extracted from all components.

- [ ] [P3] INFRA012 - Add performance monitoring utilities. Create
  src/utils/perf.ts with: measureRender(componentName) HOC that logs
  render time in dev mode, useRenderCount() hook for detecting excessive
  re-renders, throttle() and debounce() utilities. Add React.StrictMode
  in dev. Profile List rendering with 500+ items. Identify and fix
  unnecessary re-renders with React.memo where needed. Target: 60fps
  scroll performance.

=== TESTING (NEW) ===

- [ ] [P1000] TEST007 - Add unit tests for calculator.ts. Test all math
  operations: basic arithmetic, operator precedence, parentheses, functions
  (sqrt, sin, cos, etc), constants (pi, e), percentages, "X% of Y" syntax,
  edge cases (division by zero, NaN, Infinity), invalid expressions, very
  large/small numbers. Minimum 30 test cases. Use vitest.

- [ ] [P1000] TEST008 - Add unit tests for fuzzy_search.ts (after SRCH001).
  Test scoring: exact match > prefix > word boundary > substring > fuzzy.
  Test highlighting of matched characters. Test edge cases: empty query,
  empty candidates, special characters, unicode. Minimum 20 test cases.

- [ ] [P1000] TEST009 - Add unit tests for storage.ts. Test get/set/remove
  with various types (string, number, boolean, object, array). Test
  validators (isStringArray, isRecord, isBooleanRecord). Test error
  handling (invalid JSON, quota exceeded mock). Test migration paths.
  Minimum 15 test cases.

- [ ] [P1000] TEST010 - Add unit tests for format_date.ts. Test all
  relative date ranges: just now, minutes ago, hours ago, yesterday, N days
  ago, absolute date, different year. Test edge cases: midnight boundaries,
  timezone handling, future dates, invalid dates. Minimum 15 test cases.

- [ ] [P1000] TEST011 - Add integration tests for command registry. Test
  register/get/search/getSections. Test search matches title, subtitle,
  keywords, aliases. Test section ordering. Test duplicate registration
  warning. Minimum 10 test cases.

- [ ] [P1000] TEST012 - Add E2E tests with Playwright for core flows.
  Flow 1: Open → type query → select result → drill into view → Escape back.
  Flow 2: Open → ⌘K → navigate actions → execute action → verify HUD.
  Flow 3: Settings → change theme → verify visual change.
  Flow 4: Clipboard History → pin item → verify pinned section.
  Each flow as separate test. Screenshot on failure.

- [ ] [P1000] TEST013 - Add accessibility audit tests. Use axe-core with
  Playwright to audit each view for WCAG 2.1 AA violations. Test: color
  contrast ratios, ARIA roles/labels, keyboard navigation order, focus
  management, screen reader announcements. Run on: root search, clipboard
  history, snippet manager, emoji picker, settings, forms. Zero violations
  target.

- [ ] [P1000] TEST014 - Add performance benchmark tests. Measure and assert:
  initial render time < 100ms, search filter time < 50ms for 1000 items,
  navigation push/pop < 100ms, scroll FPS > 55fps with 500 items. Use
  vitest + performance.now() for unit benchmarks. Use Playwright for
  runtime performance metrics. Create performance regression baseline.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
[package]
name = "raycast-clone"
version = "0.1.0"
description = "Raycast UI clone built with Tauri"
authors = ["@antoniopresto <antoniopresto@gmail.com>"]
license = "MIT"
repository = "https://github.com/antoniopresto/lol"
edition = "2021"
rust-version = "1.77.2"

[lib]
name = "app_lib"
crate-type = ["staticlib", "cdylib", "rlib"]

[build-dependencies]
tauri-build = { version = "2", features = [] }

[dependencies]
serde_json = "1"
serde = { version = "1", features = ["derive"] }
tauri = { version = "2", features = ["tray-icon", "image-png", "macos-private-api"] }

[target.'cfg(target_os = "macos")'.dependencies]
window-vibrancy = "0.7"

[target.'cfg(any(target_os = "macos", windows, target_os = "linux"))'.dependencies]

input.prompt:
You are a senior code reviewer. Deeply review the following changes for TAURI006 - "Add native file system access via Tauri". This is a PR review simulation.

## Task Description
TAURI006 adds native file system operations to a Raycast clone built with Tauri v2 + React:
- Rust-side: `search_files`, `open_file`, `reveal_in_finder`, `move_to_trash` Tauri commands
- Platform API: `PlatformFiles` interface added to the TypeScript platform abstraction layer
- FileSearchView: refactored to use real file system in Tauri mode, mock data in web mode
- Loading state during file search
- Error handling with HUD notifications

## Files Changed
Review ALL of these files thoroughly:

1. `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/lol/src-tauri/src/lib.rs` - Rust backend (search_files, open_file, reveal_in_finder, move_to_trash commands, FileEntryResult struct, detect_file_type, is_ignored_dir)
2. `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/lol/src-tauri/Cargo.toml` - Added walkdir, dirs-next, open, tauri-plugin-shell
3. `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/lol/src-tauri/capabilities/default.json` - Added shell:allow-open permission
4. `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/lol/src/platform/types.ts` - Added FileSearchResult, PlatformFiles interfaces
5. `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/lol/src/platform/tauri.ts` - PlatformFiles Tauri implementation
6. `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/lol/src/platform/web.ts` - PlatformFiles web noop implementation
7. `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/lol/src/platform/index.ts` - Added new type exports
8. `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/lol/src/components/file_search/file_search_view.tsx` - Refactored to use platform API

## Review Criteria
1. **Correctness**: Logic bugs, race conditions, null safety
2. **Security**: Path traversal, command injection in Rust commands, URL validation
3. **Architecture**: Consistency with existing codebase patterns
4. **Performance**: N+1 issues, unnecessary allocations, search efficiency
5. **Error Handling**: Missing error paths, silent failures
6. **Type Safety**: Type correctness, missing validations
7. **Code Quality**: Dead code, unnecessary complexity, naming
8. **Rust-specific**: Memory safety, proper error handling, idiomatic Rust
9. **React-specific**: Stale closures, missing deps in useEffect/useCallback, cleanup

Provide specific actionable feedback with file paths and line references.

content.text:
---

# TAURI006 Code Review: Native File System Access

## CRITICAL: Security Vulnerabilities

### 1. Command Injection in `move_to_trash` (Severity: HIGH)

**File:** `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/lol/src-tauri/src/lib.rs`, lines 350-362

```rust
let script = format!(
    "tell application \"Finder\" to delete POSIX file \"{}\"",
    path.replace('\"', "\\\"")
);
std::process::Command::new("osascript")
    .arg("-e")
    .arg(&script)
    .output()
    .map_err(|e| format!("failed to trash {path}: {e}"))?;
```

The `path.replace('\"', "\\\"")` escaping is insufficient for AppleScript. A path containing backslash-quote sequences or other AppleScript metacharacters can break out of the string context. For example, a path like `/tmp/foo\" & do shell script \"rm -rf /` would survive the naive replace and execute arbitrary shell commands via AppleScript.

**Fix:** Use the `trash` crate (cross-platform, handles all platforms correctly) instead of hand-rolling shell escaping. Alternatively, validate the path is a canonical absolute path with no special characters, or use `NSFileManager` via objc bindings.

### 2. Path Traversal: No Input Validation on Any Path Parameter (Severity: HIGH)

**File:** `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/lol/src-tauri/src/lib.rs`, lines 306, 311, 350

All four commands (`search_files`, `open_file`, `reveal_in_finder`, `move_to_trash`) accept arbitrary `String` paths from the frontend with zero validation. A compromised or malicious frontend (e.g., via XSS in a webview) could:
- `open_file("../../etc/passwd")` -- open arbitrary files
- `move_to_trash("/etc")` -- trash system directories
- `search_files` with `paths: vec!["/"]` -- enumerate the entire filesystem

**Fix:** At minimum:
- Canonicalize paths and verify they fall under allowed directories (home dir subtree).
- Reject paths containing `..` segments.
- For `move_to_trash`, verify the file is owned by the current user and is under `$HOME`.

### 3. `open_file` Uses `open::that` Without Validation (Severity: MEDIUM)

**File:** `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/lol/src-tauri/src/lib.rs`, line 307

```rust
fn open_file(path: String) -> Result<(), String> {
    open::that(&path).map_err(|e| format!("failed to open {path}: {e}"))
}
```

`open::that` will open **any** URI scheme, not just file paths. If the frontend passes `"https://evil.com/malware.dmg"` or a `file://` URI pointing somewhere sensitive, it gets opened. This needs path validation -- confirm it's an existing file system path, not a URL.

---

## CRITICAL: Correctness Bugs

### 4. `search_files` Blocks the Main Thread (Severity: HIGH)

**File:** `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/lol/src-tauri/src/lib.rs`, line 215

```rust
#[tauri::command]
fn search_files(query: String, paths: Vec<String>, max_results: usize) -> Vec<FileEntryResult> {
```

This is a synchronous Tauri command that walks the filesystem (potentially traversing tens of thousands of entries with `max_depth(5)` across 5 root paths including `$HOME`). This **blocks the Tauri IPC thread**. While Tauri v2 runs commands on a thread pool, a synchronous file walk can still saturate the pool and block other commands.

**Fix:** Make this `async` and use `tauri::command` with `#[tauri::command(async)]` explicitly, or better, spawn it on a `tokio::task::spawn_blocking`. Consider adding a cancellation mechanism via a channel so the frontend can abort stale searches.

### 5. Race Condition: Stale Search Results in Frontend (Severity: MEDIUM)

**File:** `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/lol/src/components/file_search/file_search_view.tsx`, lines 155-175

The `searchRequestRef` counter pattern is correct for preventing stale results from overwriting newer ones. However, there is **no debounce** on the search. Every keystroke fires a full filesystem walk. If a user types "hello", that's 5 concurrent filesystem walks racing.

**Fix:** Add a debounce (200-300ms) before triggering the search. The `useRef` counter handles ordering, but the wasted I/O is severe.

### 6. `search_files` Default Paths Include Home Directory (Severity: MEDIUM)

**File:** `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/lol/src-tauri/src/lib.rs`, lines 220-229

```rust
vec![
    format!("{home_str}/Documents"),
    format!("{home_str}/Downloads"),
    format!("{home_str}/Desktop"),
    format!("{home_str}/Projects"),
    home_str,  // <-- the entire home directory
]
```

The list includes specific subdirectories AND the home directory itself. Since `Documents`, `Downloads`, `Desktop`, and `Projects` are subdirectories of home, every file in those directories will be visited **twice** -- once via the specific path and again via the home walk. This doubles the work and produces **duplicate entries** in results.

**Fix:** Either search only the specific subdirectories (remove `home_str`), or search only the home directory. Not both.

### 7. `move_to_trash` on Windows Does Not Move to Trash (Severity: MEDIUM)

**File:** `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/lol/src-tauri/src/lib.rs`, lines 374-378

```rust
#[cfg(target_os = "windows")]
{
    std::fs::remove_file(&path).map_err(|e| format!("failed to delete {path}: {e}"))?;
}
```

This **permanently deletes** the file instead of moving to trash. The function name `move_to_trash` and the UI text "You can restore this item from the Trash" are lies on Windows. Additionally, `remove_file` won't work on directories.

**Fix:** Use the `trash` crate, which correctly sends to the Recycle Bin on Windows, Trash on macOS, and freedesktop trash on Linux.

### 8. `move_to_trash` Ignores Command Exit Status (Severity: MEDIUM)

**File:** `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/lol/src-tauri/src/lib.rs`, lines 357-361

```rust
std::process::Command::new("osascript")
    .arg("-e")
    .arg(&script)
    .output()
    .map_err(|e| format!("failed to trash {path}: {e}"))?;
```

`.output()` only fails if the process fails to **spawn**. If `osascript` runs but AppleScript reports an error (e.g., file doesn't exist), `.output()` succeeds and the function returns `Ok(())`. The exit status and stderr are never checked.

Same issue with `reveal_in_finder` using `.spawn()` -- the process could fail after spawning and the Rust side would never know.

**Fix:** Check `output.status.success()` and inspect `output.stderr` when it fails.

---

## Architecture and Code Quality

### 9. `is_ignored_dir` Incorrectly Filters `.DS_Store` (Severity: LOW)

**File:** `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/lol/src-tauri/src/lib.rs`, line 205

`.DS_Store` is a **file**, not a directory. It will never match in this function since the function is only called when `entry.file_type().is_dir()` is true (line 251). The `.DS_Store` entry in `is_ignored_dir` is dead code that gives a false sense of coverage.

Additionally, `.DS_Store` files are already filtered by the `name.starts_with('.')` check on line 248. So this is doubly dead.

### 10. Sequential ID Generation: `fs-{index}` (Severity: MEDIUM)

**File:** `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/lol/src-tauri/src/lib.rs`, line 289

```rust
let id = format!("fs-{}", results.len());
```

IDs are `fs-0`, `fs-1`, etc., based on position in results. After a `moveToTrash` removes an entry from the frontend state, then a new search returns fresh results, IDs will collide with the previous set. React key reconciliation will break -- old DOM nodes will be reused for new data.

**Fix:** Use a content-based ID, e.g., a hash of the path, or use the path itself as the ID (paths are unique on a filesystem).

### 11. Inconsistent Error Icons in HUD (Severity: LOW)

**File:** `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/lol/src/components/file_search/file_search_view.tsx`, lines 257-260

```tsx
.catch(() => {
  showHUD({
    icon: <CopyHUDIcon />,  // <-- copy icon for file open failure?
    title: `Failed to open ${selectedEntry.name}`,
  });
});
```

Error states use `CopyHUDIcon` which is semantically wrong. This appears throughout the file for error cases (lines 257, 288, 345, 349). There should be an error icon, or at minimum the icon should not be `CopyHUDIcon`.

### 12. `selectedEntry` Can Be Stale Inside Callbacks (Severity: LOW)

**File:** `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/lol/src/components/file_search/file_search_view.tsx`, lines 244-271

The `handleOpen` callback captures `selectedEntry` in its dependency array, which is correct. However, `selectedEntry` is derived from `flatItems[selectedIndex]`, and both `flatItems` and `selectedIndex` can change between renders. The snapshot at callback creation time is fine for the immediate call, but the `.then()` callback (line 250-255) references `selectedEntry.name` which is captured at closure creation time -- this is actually correct since `.then()` uses the same closure's `selectedEntry`. Just noting this was checked.

### 13. `reveal_in_finder` Name Is Platform-Specific (Severity: LOW)

**File:** `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/lol/src-tauri/src/lib.rs`, line 311; `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/lol/src/platform/types.ts`, line 48

The command is named `reveal_in_finder` but it supports Linux (`xdg-open`) and Windows (`explorer`). Consider `reveal_in_file_manager` for the Rust command, though since this is a Raycast clone (macOS-first), this is acceptable if cross-platform is not a goal.

---

## Performance

### 14. No Search Debounce (Severity: HIGH)

**File:** `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/lol/src/components/file_search/file_search_view.tsx`, line 175

```tsx
}, [query]);
```

Every keystroke fires a filesystem walk. For a search bar, this is unacceptable. The `useRef` counter prevents displaying stale results, but it does not prevent the I/O load. Walking `$HOME` with `max_depth(5)` is not cheap, and doing it on every character makes the app feel sluggish and wastes system resources.

### 15. String Allocation in Hot Loop (Severity: LOW)

**File:** `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/lol/src-tauri/src/lib.rs`, lines 262-295

Inside the walker loop, `to_string_lossy().to_string()` is called multiple times per entry, and `file_type.to_string()` converts a `&'static str` to `String` unnecessarily. The struct could store `&'static str` via a lifetime parameter, but since serde serialization needs owned data anyway, this is acceptable -- just noting it's a hot allocation path.

---

## Missing Functionality

### 16. Web Platform `searchFiles` Returns Empty Array

**File:** `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/lol/src/platform/web.ts`, lines 57-59

In web mode, `files.searchFiles()` returns `[]`, but the component initializes with `MOCK_FILE_ENTRIES` and filters client-side. This means the `PlatformFiles.searchFiles` contract is not actually used in web mode -- the component bypasses it entirely. This is a leaky abstraction: the component has `isTauri` branching everywhere instead of the platform layer handling it.

**Fix (future consideration):** The web implementation of `searchFiles` could return mock entries filtered by query, making the component platform-agnostic. This would eliminate all `isTauri` checks from the component.

---

## Summary of Required Changes Before Merge

| Priority | Issue | Action |
|----------|-------|--------|
| P0 | Command injection in `move_to_trash` | Use `trash` crate |
| P0 | No path validation on any command | Add path canonicalization and scope checks |

## Task Description

## Files Changed
Review ALL of these files thoroughly:

1. `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/lol/src-tauri/src/lib.rs` - Rust backend (search_files, open_file, reveal_in_finder, move_to_trash commands, FileEntryResult struct, detect_file_type, is_ignored_dir)

content.text:
---

# TAURI006 Code Review: Native File System Access

## CRITICAL: Security Vulnerabilities

### 1. Command Injection in `move_to_trash` (Severity: HIGH)

**File:** `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/lol/src-tauri/src/lib.rs`, lines 350-362

```rust
let script = format!(
    "tell application \"Finder\" to delete POSIX file \"{}\"",
    path.replace('\"', "\\\"")
);
std::process::Command::new("osascript")
    .arg("-e")
    .arg(&script)
    .output()
    .map_err(|e| format!("failed to trash {path}: {e}"))?;
```

The `path.replace('\"', "\\\"")` escaping is insufficient for AppleScript. A path containing backslash-quote sequences or other AppleScript metacharacters can break out of the string context. For example, a path like `/tmp/foo\" & do shell script \"rm -rf /` would survive the naive replace and execute arbitrary shell commands via AppleScript.

**Fix:** Use the `trash` crate (cross-platform, handles all platforms correctly) instead of hand-rolling shell escaping. Alternatively, validate the path is a canonical absolute path with no special characters, or use `NSFileManager` via objc bindings.

### 2. Path Traversal: No Input Validation on Any Path Parameter (Severity: HIGH)

**File:** `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/lol/src-tauri/src/lib.rs`, lines 306, 311, 350

All four commands (`search_files`, `open_file`, `reveal_in_finder`, `move_to_trash`) accept arbitrary `String` paths from the frontend with zero validation. A compromised or malicious frontend (e.g., via XSS in a webview) could:
- `open_file("../../etc/passwd")` -- open arbitrary files
- `move_to_trash("/etc")` -- trash system directories
- `search_files` with `paths: vec!["/"]` -- enumerate the entire filesystem

**Fix:** At minimum:
- Canonicalize paths and verify they fall under allowed directories (home dir subtree).
- Reject paths containing `..` segments.
- For `move_to_trash`, verify the file is owned by the current user and is under `$HOME`.

### 3. `open_file` Uses `open::that` Without Validation (Severity: MEDIUM)

**File:** `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/lol/src-tauri/src/lib.rs`, line 307

```rust
fn open_file(path: String) -> Result<(), String> {
    open::that(&path).map_err(|e| format!("failed to open {path}: {e}"))
}
```

`open::that` will open **any** URI scheme, not just file paths. If the frontend passes `"https://evil.com/malware.dmg"` or a `file://` URI pointing somewhere sensitive, it gets opened. This needs path validation -- confirm it's an existing file system path, not a URL.

---

## CRITICAL: Correctness Bugs

### 4. `search_files` Blocks the Main Thread (Severity: HIGH)

**File:** `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/lol/src-tauri/src/lib.rs`, line 215

```rust
#[tauri::command]
fn search_files(query: String, paths: Vec<String>, max_results: usize) -> Vec<FileEntryResult> {
```

This is a synchronous Tauri command that walks the filesystem (potentially traversing tens of thousands of entries with `max_depth(5)` across 5 root paths including `$HOME`). This **blocks the Tauri IPC thread**. While Tauri v2 runs commands on a thread pool, a synchronous file walk can still saturate the pool and block other commands.

**Fix:** Make this `async` and use `tauri::command` with `#[tauri::command(async)]` explicitly, or better, spawn it on a `tokio::task::spawn_blocking`. Consider adding a cancellation mechanism via a channel so the frontend can abort stale searches.

### 5. Race Condition: Stale Search Results in Frontend (Severity: MEDIUM)

**File:** `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/lol/src/components/file_search/file_search_view.tsx`, lines 155-175

The `searchRequestRef` counter pattern is correct for preventing stale results from overwriting newer ones. However, there is **no debounce** on the search. Every keystroke fires a full filesystem walk. If a user types "hello", that's 5 concurrent filesystem walks racing.

**Fix:** Add a debounce (200-300ms) before triggering the search. The `useRef` counter handles ordering, but the wasted I/O is severe.

### 6. `search_files` Default Paths Include Home Directory (Severity: MEDIUM)

**File:** `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/lol/src-tauri/src/lib.rs`, lines 220-229

```rust
vec![
    format!("{home_str}/Documents"),
    format!("{home_str}/Downloads"),
    format!("{home_str}/Desktop"),
    format!("{home_str}/Projects"),
    home_str,  // <-- the entire home directory
]
```

The list includes specific subdirectories AND the home directory itself. Since `Documents`, `Downloads`, `Desktop`, and `Projects` are subdirectories of home, every file in those directories will be visited **twice** -- once via the specific path and again via the home walk. This doubles the work and produces **duplicate entries** in results.

**Fix:** Either search only the specific subdirectories (remove `home_str`), or search only the home directory. Not both.

### 7. `move_to_trash` on Windows Does Not Move to Trash (Severity: MEDIUM)

**File:** `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/lol/src-tauri/src/lib.rs`, lines 374-378

```rust
#[cfg(target_os = "windows")]
{
    std::fs::remove_file(&path).map_err(|e| format!("failed to delete {path}: {e}"))?;
}
```

This **permanently deletes** the file instead of moving to trash. The function name `move_to_trash` and the UI text "You can restore this item from the Trash" are lies on Windows. Additionally, `remove_file` won't work on directories.

**Fix:** Use the `trash` crate, which correctly sends to the Recycle Bin on Windows, Trash on macOS, and freedesktop trash on Linux.

### 8. `move_to_trash` Ignores Command Exit Status (Severity: MEDIUM)

**File:** `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/lol/src-tauri/src/lib.rs`, lines 357-361

```rust
std::process::Command::new("osascript")
    .arg("-e")
    .arg(&script)
    .output()
    .map_err(|e| format!("failed to trash {path}: {e}"))?;
```

`.output()` only fails if the process fails to **spawn**. If `osascript` runs but AppleScript reports an error (e.g., file doesn't exist), `.output()` succeeds and the function returns `Ok(())`. The exit status and stderr are never checked.

Same issue with `reveal_in_finder` using `.spawn()` -- the process could fail after spawning and the Rust side would never know.

**Fix:** Check `output.status.success()` and inspect `output.stderr` when it fails.

---

## Architecture and Code Quality

### 9. `is_ignored_dir` Incorrectly Filters `.DS_Store` (Severity: LOW)

**File:** `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/lol/src-tauri/src/lib.rs`, line 205

`.DS_Store` is a **file**, not a directory. It will never match in this function since the function is only called when `entry.file_type().is_dir()` is true (line 251). The `.DS_Store` entry in `is_ignored_dir` is dead code that gives a false sense of coverage.

Additionally, `.DS_Store` files are already filtered by the `name.starts_with('.')` check on line 248. So this is doubly dead.

### 10. Sequential ID Generation: `fs-{index}` (Severity: MEDIUM)

**File:** `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/lol/src-tauri/src/lib.rs`, line 289

```rust
let id = format!("fs-{}", results.len());
```

IDs are `fs-0`, `fs-1`, etc., based on position in results. After a `moveToTrash` removes an entry from the frontend state, then a new search returns fresh results, IDs will collide with the previous set. React key reconciliation will break -- old DOM nodes will be reused for new data.

**Fix:** Use a content-based ID, e.g., a hash of the path, or use the path itself as the ID (paths are unique on a filesystem).

### 11. Inconsistent Error Icons in HUD (Severity: LOW)

**File:** `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/lol/src/components/file_search/file_search_view.tsx`, lines 257-260

```tsx
.catch(() => {
  showHUD({
    icon: <CopyHUDIcon />,  // <-- copy icon for file open failure?
    title: `Failed to open ${selectedEntry.name}`,
  });
});
```

Error states use `CopyHUDIcon` which is semantically wrong. This appears throughout the file for error cases (lines 257, 288, 345, 349). There should be an error icon, or at minimum the icon should not be `CopyHUDIcon`.

### 12. `selectedEntry` Can Be Stale Inside Callbacks (Severity: LOW)

**File:** `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/lol/src/components/file_search/file_search_view.tsx`, lines 244-271

The `handleOpen` callback captures `selectedEntry` in its dependency array, which is correct. However, `selectedEntry` is derived from `flatItems[selectedIndex]`, and both `flatItems` and `selectedIndex` can change between renders. The snapshot at callback creation time is fine for the immediate call, but the `.then()` callback (line 250-255) references `selectedEntry.name` which is captured at closure creation time -- this is actually correct since `.then()` uses the same closure's `selectedEntry`. Just noting this was checked.

### 13. `reveal_in_finder` Name Is Platform-Specific (Severity: LOW)

**File:** `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/lol/src-tauri/src/lib.rs`, line 311; `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/lol/src/platform/types.ts`, line 48

The command is named `reveal_in_finder` but it supports Linux (`xdg-open`) and Windows (`explorer`). Consider `reveal_in_file_manager` for the Rust command, though since this is a Raycast clone (macOS-first), this is acceptable if cross-platform is not a goal.

---

## Performance

### 14. No Search Debounce (Severity: HIGH)

**File:** `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/lol/src/components/file_search/file_search_view.tsx`, line 175

```tsx
}, [query]);
```

Every keystroke fires a filesystem walk. For a search bar, this is unacceptable. The `useRef` counter prevents displaying stale results, but it does not prevent the I/O load. Walking `$HOME` with `max_depth(5)` is not cheap, and doing it on every character makes the app feel sluggish and wastes system resources.

### 15. String Allocation in Hot Loop (Severity: LOW)

**File:** `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/lol/src-tauri/src/lib.rs`, lines 262-295

Inside the walker loop, `to_string_lossy().to_string()` is called multiple times per entry, and `file_type.to_string()` converts a `&'static str` to `String` unnecessarily. The struct could store `&'static str` via a lifetime parameter, but since serde serialization needs owned data anyway, this is acceptable -- just noting it's a hot allocation path.

---

## Missing Functionality

### 16. Web Platform `searchFiles` Returns Empty Array

**File:** `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/lol/src/platform/web.ts`, lines 57-59

In web mode, `files.searchFiles()` returns `[]`, but the component initializes with `MOCK_FILE_ENTRIES` and filters client-side. This means the `PlatformFiles.searchFiles` contract is not actually used in web mode -- the component bypasses it entirely. This is a leaky abstraction: the component has `isTauri` branching everywhere instead of the platform layer handling it.

**Fix (future consideration):** The web implementation of `searchFiles` could return mock entries filtered by query, making the component platform-agnostic. This would eliminate all `isTauri` checks from the component.

---

## Summary of Required Changes Before Merge

| Priority | Issue | Action |
|----------|-------|--------|
| P0 | Command injection in `move_to_trash` | Use `trash` crate |
| P0 | No path validation on any command | Add path canonicalization and scope checks |
| P0 | `open_file` opens arbitrary URIs | Validate path is a file, not a URL |
| P1 | No search debounce | Add 200-300ms debounce |
| P1 | Duplicate traversal (home + subdirs) | Remove `home_str` from default paths |
| P1 | Windows `move_to_trash` permanently deletes | Use `trash` crate |
| P1 | Exit status not checked on shell commands | Check `status.success()` |
| P1 | `search_files` blocking behavior | Add `spawn_blocking` or async |
| P2 | Non-unique IDs (`fs-{index}`) | Use path-based IDs |
| P2 | Wrong error icons in HUD | Add/use proper error icon |
| P2 | `is_ignored_dir` has dead `.DS_Store` entry | Remove it |

This PR has strong foundational structure -- the platform abstraction layer is clean, the TypeScript types are well-defined, and the race condition handling via `searchRequestRef` is correctly implemented. However, the security issues around unvalidated paths and the AppleScript injection in `move_to_trash` are blockers that must be resolved before merge.
Excellent review. Let me address the critical and P1 issues identified.
